  Read: FULCRUM_KEY_FINDINGS.txt sections 1-3

  → FULCRUM_QUICK_REFERENCE.md "Extending Fulcrum for Omega" (reference)

KEY THINGS TO KNOW RIGHT NOW
================================================================================

1. FULCRUM = AI Agent Orchestration System
   • Kanban board for task management
   • Per-task isolated workspaces (worktrees + scratch dirs)
   • Multiple agents (Claude Code, OpenCode, and extensible for Omega)
   • 100+ MCP tools available
   • React19 + Hono on Bun + SQLite

2. ALREADY SUPPORTS MULTIPLE AGENTS
   • Claude Code via plugin hooks
   • OpenCode via SDK events
   • Both proven patterns - Omega can follow same approach
   • NO major refactoring needed

3. AGENT WORKSPACES ARE ISOLATED
   • Git worktrees: Separate branch per task
   • Scratch dirs: Non-git work
   • dtach: Persistent sessions survive server restart
   • This is the key innovation enabling parallel agent work

4. STATUS SYNC IS THE KEY CONNECTION
   • Agent becomes idle → triggers update
   • Updates task status in Fulcrum → UI changes in real-time
   • Works through plugin hooks / SDK events / MCP tools
   • Pattern: Hook/Event → CLI → API → Database → WebSocket → UI

5. MCP TOOLS ARE AGENT-AGNOSTIC
   • 100+ tools already available
   • All work for any agent
   • No changes needed for Omega - it gets full access automatically
   • Tool discovery via search_tools (deferred loading)

6. INTEGRATION IS STRAIGHTFORWARD
   • Study OpenCode implementation (proven pattern)
   • Create OmegaChatService (~200 LOC)
   • Add status tracker (~100 LOC)
   • Create plugin/hooks (~100 LOC)
   • Total: ~1000 LOC, 3-4 days, no major refactoring

INTEGRATION AT A GLANCE
================================================================================

STATUS: Fulcrum is 100% ready for Omega integration
NO major architecture changes needed
Just follow OpenCode pattern (proven to work)

EFFORT: 3-4 days
  Day 1: Design + Research (4-6 hours)
  Day 2: OmegaChatService + Routes (4-6 hours)  
  Day 3: Status Tracking + Plugin (4-6 hours)
  Day 4: Integration + Testing (4-6 hours)

APPROACH: Copy OpenCode, replace SDK
  1. Create OmegaChatService (echo opencode-chat-service.ts)
  2. Create status tracker (echo opencode plugin pattern)
  3. Add provider checks in routes
  4. Update database schema (add 'omega' to enums)
  5. Test with existing MCP tools (they all work automatically)

RESULT: Omega has full access to:
  ✅ 100+ MCP tools
  ✅ Task management + dependencies
  ✅ Messaging (WhatsApp, Slack, Discord, etc.)
  ✅ Memory store + knowledge base
  ✅ Calendar integration
  ✅ File attachments
  ✅ Notifications
  ✅ Terminal workspaces
  ✅ And everything else in Fulcrum

      ├── cli/ (MCP tools)
      ├── plugins/ (Claude + OpenCode plugins)
      ├── drizzle/ (database migrations)
      └── ... (other files)
